title: Disaster Recovery Strategies on AWS
description:
## RTO, RPO, and the Four DR Strategies

Disaster recovery is about restoring service after a major failure (an AZ outage, a Region impairment, data corruption). Two metrics drive every DR decision:

| Metric | Definition | Question it answers |
|---|---|---|
| **RTO** (Recovery Time Objective) | The maximum acceptable **time to recover** — how long the system can be down. | "How fast must we be back up?" |
| **RPO** (Recovery Point Objective) | The maximum acceptable **data loss**, expressed as a window of time. | "How much recent data can we afford to lose?" |

> Lower RTO/RPO ⇒ faster recovery / less data loss ⇒ **more standing infrastructure ⇒ higher cost.** DR is fundamentally a cost-vs-recovery trade-off.

### The four strategies (cheapest/slowest → costliest/fastest)

| Strategy | How it works | Typical RTO | Typical RPO | Relative cost |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Backup & Restore** | Back up data (and AMIs/IaC) to another Region; provision everything from scratch on disaster. | Hours | Hours | $ (lowest) |
| **Pilot Light** | Core data is continuously replicated and a minimal always-on core (e.g. a database) runs in the DR Region; the rest is switched on when needed. | Tens of minutes | Minutes | $$ |
| **Warm Standby** | A **scaled-down but fully functional** copy of the workload runs in the DR Region; scale it up and shift traffic on disaster. | Minutes | Seconds–minutes | $$$ |
| **Multi-Site Active/Active** | Full production capacity runs in **two or more Regions simultaneously**, serving live traffic; failover is near-instant. | Near-zero | Near-zero | $$$$ (highest) |

### Choosing

- Start from the **business** RTO/RPO and budget, then pick the cheapest strategy that meets both.
- **Backup & Restore**: tolerant of hours of downtime/loss (e.g. internal tools).
- **Pilot Light**: keep the irreplaceable part (data) hot, rebuild the rest.
- **Warm Standby**: need recovery in minutes with minimal data loss but want to avoid paying for full duplicate capacity.
- **Active/Active**: cannot tolerate meaningful downtime or data loss; willing to pay for and operate full redundancy (plus the complexity of multi-Region data consistency).

### Don't forget

Replicate **infrastructure as code** (CloudFormation/Terraform) and AMIs/images alongside data, and **test failover regularly** — an untested DR plan is a guess, not a plan.
